old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-8r0b","title":"TUI _observe_messages_loop catches all exceptions but may swallow important errors","description":"File: python/src/lichen/tui/native.py
Lines: 2525-2541

The _observe_messages_loop has a broad exception handler:

    async def _observe_messages_loop(self) -> None:
        try:
            if self.client is None:
                ...
                return
            subscription = await self.client.observe_inbox(self.inbox_path)
            try:
                async for messages in subscription.messages():
                    self._sync_compose_from_inputs_if_available()
                    self.apply_inbound_messages(tuple(messages))
            finally:
                await subscription.close()
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            raise
        except Exception as exc:
            self._set_messaging(self._messaging_error(str(exc)))

The 'except Exception' catches everything including:
- SystemExit (should propagate)
- KeyboardInterrupt (should propagate) 
- GeneratorExit (should propagate)

While asyncio.CancelledError is re-raised, other critical exceptions are swallowed and just set an error message.

Similar pattern in _logs_loop (2397-2418) and _raw_rx_loop (2358-2385).

Impact: Critical system exceptions may be hidden, making debugging difficult.

Fix: Use more specific exception types or re-raise BaseException:
    except asyncio.CancelledError:
        raise
    except BaseException as exc:
        if isinstance(exc, (SystemExit, KeyboardInterrupt, GeneratorExit)):
            raise
        self._set_messaging(self._messaging_error(str(exc)))","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:30:43.232841Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:30:43.232874Z"}

new_value: Fixed broad Exception handlers in _observe_messages_loop, _raw_rx_loop, and _logs_loop to properly re-raise SystemExit, KeyboardInterrupt, and GeneratorExit via BaseException check. Updated 3 locations in python/src/lichen/tui/native.py. Verified with grep for exception patterns and pytest -q python/tests/ (all passing). No test changes needed as this is defensive programming.

Lines: 2525-2541

The _observe_messages_loop has a broad exception handler:

    async def _observe_messages_loop(self) -> None:
        try:
            if self.client is None:
                ...
                return
            subscription = await self.client.observe_inbox(self.inbox_path)
            try:
                async for messages in subscription.messages():
                    self._sync_compose_from_inputs_if_available()
                    self.apply_inbound_messages(tuple(messages))
            finally:
                await subscription.close()
        except asyncio.CancelledError:
            raise
        except Exception as exc:
            self._set_messaging(self._messaging_error(str(exc)))

The 'except Exception' catches everything including:
- SystemExit (should propagate)
- KeyboardInterrupt (should propagate) 
- GeneratorExit (should propagate)

While asyncio.CancelledError is re-raised, other critical exceptions are swallowed and just set an error message.

Similar pattern in _logs_loop (2397-2418) and _raw_rx_loop (2358-2385).

Impact: Critical system exceptions may be hidden, making debugging difficult.

Fix: Use more specific exception types or re-raise BaseException:
    except asyncio.CancelledError:
        raise
    except BaseException as exc:
        if isinstance(exc, (SystemExit, KeyboardInterrupt, GeneratorExit)):
            raise
        self._set_messaging(self._messaging_error(str(exc)))","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:30:43.232841Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T19:45:46.517628199Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T19:34:22.268684792Z"}
